description.task: Implement a RowMapper interface that converts a database row (represented as a Map) into a Java object. Use it in a query method to map multiple rows.
instructions: Implement EmployeeRowMapper.mapRow() by extracting values from the row map using proper casting.
Implement MockJdbcTemplate.query() by iterating simulatedData, calling mapper.mapRow() for each row with index, and collecting results.
Output should show the SQL and all 3 employees.
description.hint: Spring's JdbcTemplate.query() works exactly like this. The RowMapper converts each ResultSet row into a domain object, eliminating manual ResultSet getters.

description.task: Implement a parameterized query executor that prevents SQL injection by separating SQL logic from parameters. Simulate JdbcTemplate.update() and JdbcTemplate.queryForObject().
instructions: Implement all three SQL operations in update(): INSERT stores {name, email} in usersTable; UPDATE changes name; DELETE removes the entry.
Implement queryForObject() to look up user by id and return their name.
Output should match the expected SQL executions and table state.
description.hint: Spring's JdbcTemplate uses PreparedStatements under the hood for parameterized queries, preventing SQL injection attacks. Never concatenate SQL and user inputs!
description.expectedOutput:
SQL: INSERT INTO users VALUES (?, ?, ?)
Params: [1, Alice, alice@example.com]
SQL: INSERT INTO users VALUES (?, ?, ?)
Params: [2, Bob, bob@example.com]
SQL: UPDATE users SET name=? WHERE id=?
Params: [Alice Smith, 1]
SQL: SELECT name FROM users WHERE id=?
Params: [1]
Found name: Alice Smith
SQL: DELETE FROM users WHERE id=?
Params: [2]
Users table:
  [1] Alice Smith <alice@example.com>

description.task: Implement a batch update operation using Spring JdbcTemplate style. Process multiple records in a single batch instead of individual inserts.
description.context: JdbcTemplate.batchUpdate() is far more efficient than looping individual inserts. It sends all SQL statements in one round-trip to the database.
instructions: In batchUpdate(), iterate batchArgs. For each Object[] arg, create an Order(orderId, product, quantity) from arg[0], arg[1], arg[2] and add to ordersTable.
Print each inserted row: 'Inserting: Order[orderId, product, quantity]'.
description.hint: JdbcTemplate.batchUpdate() uses PreparedStatement.addBatch() and executeBatch() internally, making bulk inserts much faster than individual inserts in a loop.
description.expectedOutput:
Batch SQL: INSERT INTO orders VALUES (?, ?, ?)
Batch size: 4
Inserting: Order[101, Laptop, 2]
Inserting: Order[102, Mouse, 5]
Inserting: Order[103, Keyboard, 3]
Inserting: Order[104, Monitor, 1]
Inserted: 4 rows
Total orders: 4
  Order#101 - Laptop x2
  Order#102 - Mouse x5
  Order#103 - Keyboard x3
  Order#104 - Monitor x1
